◎简　　介  

　　The story of the pitcher who threw a no-hitter while tripping on acid—known by fans and nonfans alike—has become emblematic of professional baseball’s excess in the 1970s. However, that pitcher, Dock Ellis, had a career and a life that transcended one use of LSD.During a time when the insular world of baseball was clashing with the world outside, Ellis was widely known as one ...
